zh.lifeSummary: 當Jacquetta of Luxembourg, Duchess of Bedford and Countess Rivers於1415年7月5日出生在法國諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯帕-德-卡拉斯沙吉波爾-素-特爾諾時，她的父親Pierre I de Luxembourg Comte de Brienne et de Saint-Pol25歲，母親Marguerite de Baux Contesse de Brienne et de Saint Pol21歲。她於1433年4月22日與John of Lancaster 1st Duke of Bedford在法國諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯帕-德-卡拉斯泰盧阿納結婚。她去世於1472年5月30日在英國諾薩普頓史爾葛拉芙頓 雷吉享年56歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jacquetta of Luxembourg, Duchess of Bedford and Countess Rivers于1415年7月5日出生在法国諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯帕-德-卡拉斯沙吉波爾-素-特爾諾时，她的父亲Pierre I de Luxembourg Comte de Brienne et de Saint-Pol25岁，母亲Marguerite de Baux Contesse de Brienne et de Saint Pol21岁。她于1433年4月22日与John of Lancaster 1st Duke of Bedford在法国諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯帕-德-卡拉斯泰盧阿納结婚。她去世于1472年5月30日在英国諾薩普頓史爾葛拉芙頓 雷吉享年56岁。

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

variants.name: Orff
meanings: German: variant of   Orf  .

nameMeanings.name: Bedford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from the county seat of Bedfordshire, or a smaller place of the same name in Lancashire, or from Bedforth in Thornhill, Yorkshire. Both of the former are named with the Old English personal name Bēda + Old English ford ‘ford’. The name is now very common in Yorkshire as well as Bedfordshire.

nameMeanings.name: and
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Isidor, Myer.

English, German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name for a maker of hoops and bands, etc., from Middle English band, bond, Middle High German, Middle Low German bant, German Band ‘hoop, metal band, fetter, shackle’, i.e. something used for tying or binding.
English: variant of   Bond  .

variants.name: Bandt
meanings: German: variant of   Band  .

variants.name: Bandstra
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Wouter.
West Frisian: habitational name composed with Band 4 + the suffix -stra from Old Frisian sittera ‘inhabitant of’.

variants.name: Banta
meanings: West Frisian: probably a habitational name for someone from Bant, in the 17th century an island in Friesland, now a village north of Emmeloord in the Noordoostpolder (see   Band   4, compare   Banda   5). This surname is no longer found in the Netherlands.
History: Epke Jacobs Banta arrived in New Netherland in 1659.

variants.name: Bandel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Zvi, Emanuel, Hyman, Meir.
South German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): from a diminutive of   Band  . See also   Bendel  .
German: in some cases, a variant of Pandel, a pet form of the medieval personal name Pantaleon (see Pantaleo ).

variants.name: Bend
meanings: English (Leicestershire and Nottinghamshire): from Middle English bend ‘band, fetter, chain; ribbon, sash, stripe’, for someone who made, wore, or used such items. See also the Band 1 and compare Bendy 1.

variants.name: Bandler
meanings: German: occupational name for a maker of band (for vessels) or ribbon or lace, from an agent derivative of Middle High German bandel, a diminutive of bant ‘band’ (see Band ).

variants.name: Bandy
meanings: English: perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English bandi, bondi, bendi, derivatives of Middle English and Old English band, bond, bend ‘band, ribbon, stripe’ + Middle English -i, reinforced by Old French bendé ‘striped’, with reference perhaps to clothing ornamented with ribbons or stripes, or to the diagonal band of a heraldic device. Compare Band .
English: alternatively, perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English bandy, bendy ‘curved stick’, used for someone with bent or crooked legs.
Czech and Slovak: from a derivative of banda ‘(gipsy) band’, apparently a nickname for a bandsman. This surname is found among Gypsies.

variants.name: Bond
meanings: English: status name for a peasant farmer or husbandman, Middle English bond(e), bounde, occasionally bande ‘bondman, customary tenant, serf’ (Old English bonda, bunda, reinforced by Old Norse bóndi). The Old Norse word was also in use as a personal name (Old Norse Bóndi, Bondi, Bundi, Bonde, borrowed as late Old English Bonda), and this has given rise to other English and Scandinavian surnames alongside those originating as status names, such as the Middle English personal name Bonde. The status of the peasant farmer fluctuated considerably during the Middle Ages; moreover, the underlying ancient Germanic word is of disputed origin and meaning. Among ancient Germanic peoples who settled to an agricultural life, the term came to signify a farmer holding lands from, and bound by loyalty to, a lord; from this developed the sense of a free landholder as opposed to a serf. In England after the Norman Conquest the word sank in status and became associated with the notion of bound servitude. The name can also be a variant of Band .
Swedish: variant of   Bonde  .
In some cases also an American shortened form of Ukrainian Bondarenko and possibly also of some other surname beginning with Bond-.

nameMeanings.name: Countess
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Nottinghamshire, of Norman origin): from Middle English contas(e), Old French contesse ‘countess’, applied as a nickname for a proud, haughty woman or for a foppish man, or as an occupational name for a servant of a countess.

variants.name: Countiss
meanings: English: variant of   Countess  .

nameMeanings.name: Rivers
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of River , with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Americanized form (translation into English) of French Larivière (see Lariviere ).

variants.name: River
meanings: English: variant of   Rivers  .
Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) surname in some other language, such as German Riffer (see Riffey ) and Spanish Rivera .

English: topographic name from a misdivision of Middle English atter evere ‘at the brow or edge’ (Old English yfer). The surname may denote someone who lived on the slope or brow of a hill, or someone who came from River in Tillington (Sussex), or perhaps River Hill (Binstead, Hants), which have the same etymology.

nameMeanings.name: Jacquetta
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Anglicized spelling (influenced by Jacqueline ) of the Italian name Giachetta, a feminine diminutive of Giac(om)o, the Italian form of James .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Jacquetta of Luxembourg, Duchess of Bedford and Countess Rivers was born on 5 July 1415, in Saint-Pol-sur-Ternoise, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, her father, Pierre I de Luxembourg Comte de Brienne et de Saint-Pol, was 25 and her mother, Marguerite de Baux Contesse de Brienne et de Saint Pol, was 21. She married John of Lancaster 1st Duke of Bedford on 22 April 1433, in Thérouanne, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France. She died on 30 May 1472, in Grafton Regis, Northamptonshire, England, at the age of 56.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jacquetta of Luxembourg, Duchess of Bedford and Countess Rivers est née le 5 juillet 1415, à Saint-Pol-sur-Ternoise, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, son père, Pierre I de Luxembourg Comte de Brienne et de Saint-Pol, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Marguerite de Baux Contesse de Brienne et de Saint Pol, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé John of Lancaster 1st Duke of Bedford le 22 avril 1433, à Thérouanne, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France. Elle est décédée le 30 mai 1472, à Grafton Regis, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 56 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jacquetta of Luxembourg, Duchess of Bedford and Countess Rivers am 5. Juli 1415 in Saint-Pol-sur-Ternoise, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Pierre I de Luxembourg Comte de Brienne et de Saint-Pol 25 und ihre Mutter Marguerite de Baux Contesse de Brienne et de Saint Pol 21. Sie hat am 22. April 1433 in Thérouanne, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Frankreich John of Lancaster 1st Duke of Bedford geheiratet. Sie starb am 30. Mai 1472 im Alter von 56 Jahren in Grafton Regis, Northamptonshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jacquetta of Luxembourg, Duchess of Bedford and Countess Rivers è nata il il 5 luglio 1415 nel Saint-Pol-sur-Ternoise, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia, sua padre, Pierre I de Luxembourg Comte de Brienne et de Saint-Pol, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Marguerite de Baux Contesse de Brienne et de Saint Pol, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato John of Lancaster 1st Duke of Bedford il 22 aprile 1433, nel Thérouanne, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia. Morì il 30 maggio 1472, nel Grafton Regis, Northamptonshire, England, all'età di 56 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Jacquetta of Luxembourg, Duchess of Bedford and Countess Riversが1415年7月5日にフランスノード-プアス-デ-カレイスプアス-デ-カレイスサイント-プオル-スル-テルノイゼで生まれた時，父Pierre I de Luxembourg Comte de Brienne et de Saint-Polは25歳，母Marguerite de Baux Contesse de Brienne et de Saint Polは21歳であった。1433年4月22日にフランスノード-プアス-デ-カレイスプアス-デ-カレイススeロウアンでJohn of Lancaster 1st Duke of Bedfordと結婚した。彼女は1472年5月30日にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアグラフトン レジスで56歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jacquetta of Luxembourg, Duchess of Bedford and Countess Rivers(이)가 1415년 7월 5일 프랑스 노르드-파스-데-캘레이 파스-데-캘레이 세인트-포-수-터노이스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Pierre I de Luxembourg Comte de Brienne et de Saint-Pol(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Marguerite de Baux Contesse de Brienne et de Saint Pol(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1433년 4월 22일 프랑스 노르드-파스-데-캘레이 파스-데-캘레이 스e루앤에서 John of Lancaster 1st Duke of Bedford(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1472년 5월 30일 영국 노르섬프토실르 그라프턴 레지스에서 56세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jacquetta of Luxembourg, Duchess of Bedford and Countess Rivers nasceu em 5 de julho de 1415, em Saint-Pol-sur-Ternoise, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, França, seu pai, Pierre I de Luxembourg Comte de Brienne et de Saint-Pol, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Marguerite de Baux Contesse de Brienne et de Saint Pol, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com John of Lancaster 1st Duke of Bedford em 22 de abril de 1433, em Thérouanne, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, França. Ela faleceu em 30 de maio de 1472, em Grafton Regis, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, com 56 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jacquetta of Luxembourg, Duchess of Bedford and Countess Rivers родилась 5 июля 1415 г. в местности сеинт-пол-сур-тирноис, пас-де-калеи, норд-пас-де-калеи, Франция, её отцу по имени Pierre I de Luxembourg Comte de Brienne et de Saint-Pol было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Marguerite de Baux Contesse de Brienne et de Saint Pol, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John of Lancaster 1st Duke of Bedford 22 апреля 1433 г. в местности тeроуанн, пас-де-калеи, норд-пас-де-калеи, Франция. Она умерла 30 мая 1472 г. в местности графтон регис, нортамптоншир, Англия в возрасте 56 лет.
